Archetype Overview

Archetype Overview

Section titled вҖңArchetype OverviewвҖқ

Archetypes define the lifecycle, states, and skills for different types of teams in Squad Federation. Think of them as templates or roles that teams adopt.

What is an Archetype?

Section titled вҖңWhat is an Archetype?вҖқ

An archetype specifies:

  1. States - Valid lifecycle phases (e.g., scanning, complete)
  2. Skills - Knowledge base files teams can access
  3. Agents (optional) - Specialized sub-agents for the archetype
  4. Monitors (optional) - Custom monitoring logic

Built-In Archetypes

Section titled вҖңBuilt-In ArchetypesвҖқ

Squad Federation provides three built-in archetypes:

ArchetypePurposeUse Case
codingWrite code, implement featuresFeature development, bug fixes
deliverableCreate documents, reportsDocumentation, analysis reports
consultantProvide recommendationsArchitecture review, code review

Archetype Directory Structure

Section titled вҖңArchetype Directory StructureвҖқ

Root Manifest

Section titled вҖңRoot ManifestвҖқ

File: archetypes/archetype.json

Purpose: Central registry of all available archetypes

Fields:

  • archetypes - Map of archetype ID вҶ’ paths
  • path - Directory containing archetype files
  • archetypeJson - Path to archetype config file

Archetype Config

Section titled вҖңArchetype ConfigвҖқ

File: {archetype-path}/archetype.json

Example (coding):

Fields:

FieldTypeDescription
archetypeIdstringUnique identifier (matches root manifest key)
namestringDisplay name
statesstring[]Valid lifecycle states
skillsstring[]Skill file paths (relative to archetype dir)

States

Section titled вҖңStatesвҖқ

States represent team lifecycle phases.

Common States

Section titled вҖңCommon StatesвҖқ
StateDescriptionTransitions
initializingTeam starting upвҶ’ scanning
scanningAnalyzing codebaseвҶ’ distilling, failed, paused
distillingProcessing findingsвҶ’ complete, failed, paused
completeWork finished(terminal)
failedError occurred(terminal)
pausedManually pausedвҶ’ any previous state

State Machine Example (Coding)

Section titled вҖңState Machine Example (Coding)вҖқ

Custom States

Section titled вҖңCustom StatesвҖқ

Archetypes can define custom states:

Teams track their current state in .squad/status.json:

Skills

Section titled вҖңSkillsвҖқ

Skills are markdown files containing knowledge, patterns, or guidelines.

Structure:

Metadata:

  • tags - Keywords for search
  • category - Skill type (pattern, discovery, convention, gotcha)
  • source - Originating team (if graduated from learning)
  • promoted - Graduation date

Purpose:

  • Provide team-specific knowledge base
  • Share best practices across teams
  • Document conventions and patterns

Agents

Section titled вҖңAgentsвҖқ

Archetypes can include custom agents for specialized tasks.

Example: team/agents/lead.md

Purpose:

  • Delegate work to specialized agents
  • Customize agent behavior per archetype
  • Provide role-specific instructions

Monitors

Section titled вҖңMonitorsвҖқ

Archetypes can provide custom monitoring logic.

Example: team/monitors/coding-monitor.ts

Purpose:

  • Validate team progress
  • Emit custom metrics
  • Provide archetype-specific health checks

Choosing an Archetype

Section titled вҖңChoosing an ArchetypeвҖқ

When to use coding

Section titled вҖңWhen to use codingвҖқ
  • Implementing features
  • Fixing bugs
  • Refactoring code
  • Writing tests

Output: Code changes, commits

When to use deliverable

Section titled вҖңWhen to use deliverableвҖқ
  • Writing documentation
  • Creating reports
  • Generating analysis
  • Producing artifacts (non-code)

Output: Markdown files, reports, documentation

When to use consultant

Section titled вҖңWhen to use consultantвҖқ
  • Reviewing code
  • Providing recommendations
  • Architecture guidance
  • Answering questions

Output: Recommendations, findings, advice (no changes)

Creating Custom Archetypes

Section titled вҖңCreating Custom ArchetypesвҖқ

See Creating Archetypes for a full guide.

Quick example:

  1. Create directory: archetypes/plugins/my-archetype/
  2. Add archetype.json:
  3. Register in archetypes/archetype.json:
  4. Onboard team:

Team Initialization

Section titled вҖңTeam InitializationвҖқ

When a team is onboarded, the archetype files are copied to the teamвҖҷs workspace:

Before onboarding:

After onboarding:

Purpose: Give each team a fresh copy of the archetype files.

Archetype vs Team

Section titled вҖңArchetype vs TeamвҖқ
ConceptScopePurpose
ArchetypeTemplateDefines what a type of team can do
TeamInstanceA specific team using an archetype

Analogy:

  • Archetype = Class definition
  • Team = Instance of that class

Example:

  • Archetype: coding (template for code-writing teams)
  • Team: frontend (specific team using coding archetype)

Next Steps

Section titled вҖңNext StepsвҖқ
  • Create custom archetypes
  • Learn about the coding archetype
  • Learn about the deliverable archetype
  • Learn about the consultant archetype
